tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a UTF-8 text file. Reads inside the project freely; outside the project, reads are allowed in a readable zone — by default the project's parent dir (so sibling repos in the same workspace are readable) plus ~/Desktop and ~/Downloads. Override with VANTA_READABLE_DIRS. Use an absolute or ~-prefixed path for files outside the repo.

path.description: Path relative to the project root, or an absolute / ~-prefixed path inside a readable zone

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Targeted string replacement in a file — replace old_string with new_string. Fails if old_string is not found or appears more than once (unless replace_all is true). Safer than write_file for precise edits to large files; does not require a full rewrite.

path.description: Path relative to project root, or absolute / ~-prefixed

old_string.description: Exact string to find and replace (must be unique unless replace_all is true)

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of failing on duplicates (default: false)

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write a UTF-8 text file. Inside the project: new files write directly. Outside the project: allowed only in a writable zone (~/Desktop, ~/Downloads, or VANTA_WRITABLE_DIRS) and always approval-gated. Overwriting an existing file requires approval. To put a file on the user's Desktop, write directly to ~/Desktop/<name> — don't write in the repo and copy.

path.description: Path relative to the project root, or an absolute / ~-prefixed path inside a writable zone (e.g. ~/Desktop/notes.md)

tools.name: grep_files
tools.description: Search file contents by regex pattern using ripgrep (rg). Returns file:line:content matches. Falls back to grep when rg is unavailable. Read-only — use instead of shell_cmd for searches.

pattern.description: Regex or fixed-string pattern to search for

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: project root)

max_results.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

tools.name: glob_files
tools.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. 'src/**/*.ts', '**/*.{json,yaml}'). Returns matching paths sorted alphabetically. Read-only.

base_path.description: Base directory to search from (default: project root)

tools.name: shell_cmd
tools.description: Run a shell command inside the project scope. Returns combined stdout/stderr. Destructive commands are blocked. Set background=true for long-running commands — returns a task id immediately.

background.description: Run in background (returns task id immediately; check with bg_status)

tools.name: inspect_state
tools.description: Inspect Vanta operating state: active goals or the approval queue. Use this to know what you are working toward.

tools.name: clarify
tools.description: Ask the user a clarifying question when their intent is ambiguous. Returns the formatted question for you to surface in your reply. Use this instead of guessing — wrong assumptions cost rework. Ask one question per turn; await the user's answer before proceeding.

question.description: The clarifying question to ask the user.

options.description: Optional structured choices. Numbered automatically. Omit for open-ended answers.

tools.name: config
tools.description: Read or write Vanta settings. 'get' returns the current value; 'set' updates a setting and persists it to .env. Only allows whitelisted keys (VANTA_*). Requires approval for writes.

action.description: Either 'get' to read a setting or 'set' to write it.

key.description: The setting key (VANTA_* env vars only).

value.description: The new value when action is 'set'. Omit to unset the key.

tools.name: cookie_import
tools.description: Store a browser-exported login cookie for a reach channel (reddit, twitter, …) so its tools can read login-walled content. Three sources: `browser:"brave"` reads your live logged-in session straight from the browser's cookie store (no export — macOS, one Keychain approval); or a Cookie-Editor JSON / Netscape cookies.txt / 'k=v' header pasted as `cookie` or read from a saved export via `file`. Stored 0600 in ~/.vanta — local only, never logged or uploaded.

browser.description: read the live session from this browser's cookie store (macOS)

cookie.description: the export contents (JSON, cookies.txt, or a header string)

file.description: path to a saved export file (alternative to cookie; supports ~)

tools.name: cron_create
tools.description: Create a scheduled task. durable=true persists to .vanta/scheduled_tasks.json.

tools.name: cron_list
tools.description: List scheduled tasks from cron.tsv and scheduled_tasks.json.

tools.name: sleep
tools.description: Pause execution for a given number of seconds. Useful for polling loops, waiting for async side-effects to complete, or rate-limit backoff.

seconds.description: Number of seconds to sleep (0–3600). Default: 1.

tools.name: roadmap_move
tools.description: Move a roadmap item to a new status. Updates roadmap.json and regenerates roadmap.html. Valid statuses: shipped, building, next, horizon.

tools.name: roadmap_add
tools.description: Add a NEW roadmap card to roadmap.json (then regenerates roadmap.html). Enforces a unique id and the card schema. Required: id, title. Defaults: status=next, track=Backlog, size=M. Use roadmap_move to change an existing card's status instead.

id.description: Unique card id, e.g. 'AUTO-HANDOFF' (refused if it already exists).

summary.description: What the card is + why (one paragraph).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web and return a numbered list of result titles, URLs, and snippets.

max_results.description: Maximum results to return (1-10). Defaults to 5.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL and return its main content as clean, readable text (markdown-ish). Strips nav, scripts, and boilerplate.

tools.name: write_skill
tools.description: Record a reusable skill learned from experience so it can be recalled and applied later.

name.description: Short kebab-friendly name for the skill

description.description: One-line summary of what the skill does

body.description: The markdown how-to that captures the skill

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Load the full body of the most relevant learned skill for a task. The skill INDEX (names + descriptions) is already in your system prompt; use recall to pull the actual step-by-step know-how of one before applying it.

query.description: What you need help with — matched against skill names and descriptions.

tools.name: reddit_read
tools.description: Search Reddit or read a post + its top comments. action:search {query, subreddit?, limit?} finds posts; action:read {url} reads a post permalink + comments. Uses Reddit's .json API with your stored cookie; if that's blocked (403 / no cookie), FALLS BACK to rendering the page in a real browser with your session. Source-cited.

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Capture a full-page PNG screenshot of an approved URL, saving it to a path inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to save the .png, relative to the project root

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Open a URL in a headless browser, run a short sequence of actions (click, fill, scroll), and return the resulting page's visible text.

actions.description: Ordered actions to perform after the page loads

tools.name: browser_extract
tools.description: Load a URL in a headless browser and extract its text, links, or tables. Domains outside the allowlist require approval.

tools.name: browser_act
tools.description: Drive a browser page — navigate, click, type, press a key, scroll, or wait. Irreversible actions (submit, buy, delete, login, send) and credential entry stop and ask first. Returns the resulting page's visible text. Set observe:true to also return a numbered list of interactable elements (links, buttons, inputs) with suggested selectors — use this to ground the next click before issuing it. Pass a `secret:true` flag on a type action to mask + gate it. Disabled when VANTA_BROWSER_DISABLED is set.

text.description: click: visible text to match (alternative to selector)

secret.description: type: mask the value + treat as risky (credential)

observe.description: When true, append a numbered list of the page's interactable elements after the body text. Use this to identify selectors before clicking. Default false.

tools.name: browser_read
tools.description: Read ANY web page through a real headless browser — renders JS and follows your logged-in session. Pass browser:"brave" to auto-inject your logged-in cookies for the page's domain, so it reads login-walled / JS-rendered pages (x.com, reddit, linkedin, internal apps, …) that plain web_fetch can't. Returns the page's visible text. Works for every site — not specific to any one platform.

browser.description: inject your logged-in session from this browser (macOS)

tools.name: describe_image
tools.description: Send a local image to a vision model and return a text description. Reads inside the project freely; outside it, the image must be in a readable zone (the project's parent dir plus ~/Desktop and ~/Downloads by default). Use an absolute or ~-prefixed path for files outside the repo (e.g. a screenshot on ~/Desktop).

path.description: Path relative to the project root, or an absolute / ~-prefixed path inside a readable zone

prompt.description: What to look for (defaults to a general description)

tools.name: compare_vision
tools.description: Compare 1–4 images and produce a grounded visual critique referencing known brand preferences. Returns a ranked recommendation, per-image critique, and a direction note.

images.description: Paths to image files (absolute or relative to project root). 1–4 images.

focus.description: Optional evaluation dimension, e.g. 'layout hierarchy', 'brand fit', 'visual weight'.

tools.name: look_at_screen
tools.description: Capture the user's current screen and describe it with a vision model — Vanta's eyes. Use to see what the user is looking at, read on-screen content, or check the state of an app or UI.

prompt.description: What to look for (defaults to a general description)

tools.name: look_at_camera
tools.description: Capture a frame from the webcam and describe it with the active vision model (macOS, needs imagesnap).

tools.name: brain
tools.description:
Read and grow your own brain (durable, git-versioned). Regions:
identity — Who Vanta is — self-concept, personality, values, voice. Vanta evolves this from how the user works with it.
semantic — Durable facts Vanta knows about the world, the user, and the codebase. Append facts that stay true.
episodic — Distilled highlights of notable past sessions and events — what happened and why it mattered.
user_model — Vanta's evolving model of the user — preferences, working style, patterns, relationship, trust.
drives — Standing wants and what Vanta is working toward, beyond the current task.
reflections — Lessons learned, self-critique, mistakes to avoid, what Vanta is improving about itself.
mood — Vanta's current affective and operating state — kept brief.
salience — High-priority signals, urgent concerns, or context shifts that should modulate current attention — updated per session when something important surfaces.
executive — Active plans being tracked, things to actively inhibit or defer (anti-goals), and constraints on the current task stack.
Use action=list to see regions, read to load one in full, append to add what you've learned (preferred — non-destructive), replace to rewrite a region. Update user_model/semantic/episodic as you learn about the user and world; reflections after mistakes; identity/personality as it forms. For discrete memories use remember (typed entry with strength + optional forget_after decay) and recall (top memories by strength×recency; recalling reinforces them).

region.description: Brain region (see list). Required except for list/recall.

forget_after.description: remember: ISO date after which the memory decays.

tools.name: brief
tools.description: Send a structured notification message with optional file attachments. Use 'normal' for routine updates or 'proactive' for agent-initiated alerts. Files are referenced by path and rendered in the user interface.

files.description: Optional file paths to attach (relative or absolute).

tools.name: todo
tools.description: Track a multi-step plan as a checklist. action=write replaces the list with items [{text, status?}] (status: pending|in_progress|done, default pending) — plan before a complex task and keep it current as you progress. action=list returns the current plan. The user views it with /plan.

action.description: write replaces the plan; list shows it

tools.name: world
tools.description: Vanta's world model: a durable graph of entities (people, projects, repos, companies, goals, accounts, commitments) and their relationships, persisted across sessions. action:record adds/updates an entity (id, type, name, optional note/confidence); action:relate links two entities (from, to, rel like owns/depends-on/blocked-by/promised-to/next-action-for); action:query searches entities with source citations (q over type/name/note/relation); action:conflicts lists contradictions (same subject+predicate with different objects); action:duplicates suggests entity pairs with same type+name for merging; action:merge consolidates dropId into keepId (re-points relations, tombstones the drop). Use it to remember and reason about the user's systems coherently.

action.description: record | relate | query (cited) | conflicts | merge (consolidate) | duplicates (suggest merges)

type.description: person | project | repo | company | goal | account | commitment | tool | asset

rel.description: owns | depends-on | blocked-by | promised-to | relevant-to | next-action-for

q.description: query string (for query; empty = all without citations)

dropId.description: entity id to consolidate away (for merge)

tools.name: money
tools.description: Vanta's money-making ledger: track offers, prospects, revenue, deliverables, and follow-ups. Append-only JSONL, global across sessions. action:offer records a service or product (id, name, optional price/note); action:prospect records a pipeline contact (id, name, stage: lead|contacted|replied|booked|won|lost); action:revenue records an income event (amount, optional source/note); action:review summarizes total revenue, pipeline by stage, and offer count; action:price suggests a low/median/high price band (name=offer label, note=comma-separated comparables e.g. '1000,2000,3000'); action:weekly returns a weekly snapshot (revenue, open pipeline, top prospect, new offers, follow-ups due, deliverable progress); action:deliverable adds or updates a deliverable (id, title, optional prospectId/status/due; status: todo|doing|done); action:followup adds or completes a follow-up (id, prospectId, note, due ISO date; set done=ISO date to mark complete). Drafts and records only — never sends, never a fake identity.

action.description: offer | prospect | revenue | review | price | weekly | deliverable | followup

due.description: ISO date string (deliverable due, followup due)

done.description: ISO date string — set to mark followup complete

tools.name: radar
tools.description: Vanta's opportunity radar: a durable ledger of scored business opportunities, persisted across sessions. action:record adds/updates an opportunity (id, title, optional source/note); action:score sets pain (0..1 — how expensive/urgent/repeated/reachable the problem is) and/or buyer (0..1 — how reachable/budgeted/timing-ready the buyer is) on an existing opportunity (id required); action:list returns all opportunities ranked by composite score (pain + buyer, 0..2); action:scan returns a ranked scan with composite scores and position numbers; action:offer drafts a short offer pitch for a given opportunity (id required); action:promote promotes a scored opportunity into a Money-OS prospect (id required) at stage:lead. action:scan_web pulls live candidate opportunities from a reach source and appends them, scored by pain+buyer heuristics (degrades gracefully when a source is unavailable). from:web (default) searches the web (query required); from:reddit searches Reddit for pain signals (query required, optional subreddit — needs a reddit cookie); from:rss reads a feed (feed url required); from:twitter searches X/Twitter for pain signals (query required — via twitter-cli). Use it to track, score, surface, and act on the highest-signal opportunities.

action.description: record | score pain+buyer | list ranked | scan ranked | offer draft | promote to Money-OS prospect | scan_web live web scan

subreddit.description: scan_web from:reddit — limit to a subreddit (optional)

tools.name: reach
tools.description: Inspect + self-heal Vanta's internet-reach channels. action:doctor reports each channel's active backend + status + the exact fix on a gap. action:heal {channel} rebuilds a broken CLI-backed channel (e.g. re-pulls twitter-cli when X changes its API), then re-checks. Use heal when a reach channel (twitter, …) starts failing — the backend's maintainer tracks the platform's churn.

tools.name: team
tools.description: Worker roster + task ledger. action:define — add/update a worker (id, role, model?, tools?, note?); action:status — update worker status (id, status: idle|running|blocked|done); action:list — list roster; action:dispatch — assign a task to a worker (taskId, workerId, title); action:advance — move a task to a new status (taskId, taskStatus: assigned|running|done|blocked, detail?); action:tasks — list tasks (optional: workerId to filter); action:run — actually execute a dispatched task by spawning a worker agent (taskId; optional detail = instruction), updating the task to done/blocked with the result.

action.description: define worker | update worker status | list roster | dispatch task | advance task | list tasks | run task (spawn worker)

model.description: model id the worker runs on (define, optional)

tools.description: tool names (define, optional)

tools.name: life_search
tools.description: Search or refresh Vanta's local stores (world/money/radar/team JSONL + ERRORS.md). action:search (default) — keyword search, returns source-cited snippets ranked by relevance. action:semantic — embed the query and re-rank hits by cosine similarity (requires Ollama; falls back to lexical ranking with a notice if unavailable). action:refresh — recompute per-store content digests, report which stores changed since last refresh, save new digests.

q.description: keyword or phrase to search (required for action:search and action:semantic)

tools.name: linkedin_read
tools.description: Read a LinkedIn profile, company, post, or search-results page (login-walled + JS-rendered) through a real browser using your logged-in session. Pass browser:"brave" to auto-use your LinkedIn login, or cookie_import a linkedin cookie first. Returns the page's visible text. (Built on the browser-session reach capability.)

url.description: a linkedin.com URL (profile, company, post, or search)

browser.description: auto-use your logged-in session from this browser (macOS)

tools.name: regression_lock
tools.description: Lock a verified behavior so a later change can't silently break it. action:lock {claim, command, expect} records a claim + the shell command that proves it + the substring its output must contain. action:check [id] re-runs the locked command(s) and flags a regression if the substring is gone or the command fails (each run is approval-gated). action:list shows every lock and its current status.

id.description: lock id (check: limit to one; lock: optional explicit id)

command.description: lock: shell command that proves it

expect.description: lock: substring the command output must contain

tools.name: rss_read
tools.description: Read an RSS or Atom feed: fetch the feed URL and return its recent items (title, link, date, summary). Zero-config, no API key. Use it to follow blogs, subreddit feeds (…/.rss), release notes, or news.

tools.name: self_repair
tools.description: Self-repair: mark a compartment's current code as last-known-good, or roll it back to that sha. action:mark {compartment} records the current HEAD as the compartment's good state. action:rollback {compartment} restores it (git checkout of the compartment's paths) — approval-gated, refuses protected compartments (brainstem/skeleton) and discards uncommitted changes under those paths. action:sandbox_test {toolPath} runs a bounded OS-sandboxed test for a new/replaced limb tool before attach. action:status lists recorded markers. Compartments: brainstem, skeleton, reflexes, memory, limbs.

command.description: optional bounded vanta-ts test command for sandbox_test

tools.name: watch_video
tools.description: Watch a video: sample frames with ffmpeg and describe them with the active vision model. Args: path (video file), prompt (optional), frames (1-8, default 4).

tools.name: speak
tools.description: Speak text aloud via text-to-speech (macOS `say`). Use when the user asks for a spoken reply.

tools.name: swarm
tools.description: Run up to 5 scoped subtasks IN PARALLEL as worker agents, each optionally on its own model/provider, and get all results back. Use to fan a goal across workers — research three things at once, or run one task on local ollama and a harder one on gpt-4o simultaneously.

tools.name: compose_workflow
tools.description: Compose and run a multi-agent workflow with typed steps (fan-out, synthesize, adversarial-verify, tournament, loop-until-done). Each step spawns one or more sub-agents and aggregates their outputs. Use for tasks that benefit from parallelism, adversarial checking, or iterative refinement.

tools.name: graph_query
tools.description: Query the knowledge graph for entities and their relationships. Returns matching entities with their direct connections (worked-on, decided, depends-on, related-to, etc.).

tools.name: transcribe
tools.description: Transcribe an audio file to text (speech-to-text via whisper). Args: path, model (default base).

tools.name: twitter_read
tools.description: Search X/Twitter or list your bookmarks — native GraphQL, no external CLI, keyless cookie auth. action:search {query, max?, latest?} finds tweets; action:bookmarks {max?} lists your saved tweets. Needs an x.com cookie (cookie_import channel "twitter") + current query ids (reach heal twitter). Source-cited.

latest.description: search: newest first instead of top

tools.name: youtube_read
tools.description: Extract info and/or subtitles from a YouTube video via yt-dlp. mode=info returns title/description/metadata; mode=subtitles returns the caption text; mode=both (default) returns everything available. Zero-config if yt-dlp is installed.

mode.description: What to extract — both is default

tools.name: github_read
tools.description: Read GitHub repos, issues, PRs, and READMEs via gh CLI. Zero-config for public repos. Run gh auth login to unlock private repos, fork, issue, PR creation.

action.description: repo=view details, issues=list open issues, prs=list open PRs, readme=read README, search=search GitHub

repo.description: owner/repo or full GitHub URL (not needed for search)

tools.name: podcast_read
tools.description: Transcribe a podcast episode or audio file via Groq Whisper (whisper-large-v3). Pass a direct audio URL (.mp3/.m4a etc.). Requires GROQ_API_KEY (free at console.groq.com). Audio must be ≤24 MB.

tools.name: run_code
tools.description: Run a code snippet (python, node, or rust) in an isolated temp dir with a 30s timeout. Returns combined stdout/stderr. Requires approval.

language.description: The language to run the snippet in

tools.name: lsp_diagnostics
tools.description: Report TypeScript diagnostics (errors and warnings) for a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

tools.name: lsp_definition
tools.description: Find the definition site(s) of the symbol at a position in a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

character.description: Zero-based character offset of the symbol

tools.name: list_mcp_resources
tools.description: List all resources exposed by mounted MCP servers. Returns resource URIs and descriptions. Resources are file-like content provided by MCP servers (e.g., API docs, code files, logs).

tools.name: read_mcp_resource
tools.description: Read the content of a resource from a mounted MCP server. Requires the full resource URI (available via list_mcp_resources).

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show working-tree status (porcelain) with branch info.

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Show unstaged changes, optionally limited to one path.

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Stage all changes and commit with a message. Requires approval.

tools.name: git_push
tools.description: Push commits to a remote. Requires approval.

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: Create a branch by name, or list branches when no name given. Requires approval.

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Check out a branch, tag, or commit ref. Requires approval.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit to check out

tools.name: delegate
tools.description: Delegate a scoped subtask to a worker agent — optionally on a DIFFERENT model/provider. The worker runs its own loop with the same tools (minus delegate) and returns its result. Use `provider`/`model` to route a subtask to the best backend (e.g. provider:'ollama' for a free local model, provider:'openai' model:'gpt-4o' for a hard reasoning step). Call it multiple times to fan a goal out across several workers/models.

goal.description: The worker's scoped goal — the outcome to achieve

instruction.description: Concrete instructions for the worker to follow

max_iterations.description: Optional cap on the worker's loop iterations (1-50)

provider.description: Optional backend for the worker: openai | ollama | anthropic | gemini | openrouter | codex | claude-code. Defaults to the parent's.

model.description: Optional model id for the worker (e.g. gpt-4o, qwen2.5:14b, gemini-2.5-flash).

isolation.description: Set to 'worktree' to run the agent in a fresh git worktree on a new branch so parallel agents don't conflict.

tools.name: gmail_search
tools.description: Search the user's Gmail with a Gmail query string. Returns matching message ids with sender, subject, and snippet.

tools.name: gmail_read
tools.description: Read a single Gmail message by id. Returns its headers and plain-text body.

tools.name: gmail_draft
tools.description: Create a Gmail draft (does not send). Requires human approval.

tools.name: gmail_send
tools.description: Send an email from the user's account. Irreversible. Requires human approval.

tools.name: calendar_read
tools.description: List upcoming events from the user's primary Google calendar, ordered by start time.

tools.name: calendar_create
tools.description: Create an event on the user's primary Google calendar. Always requires approval.

tools.name: calendar_update
tools.description: Update fields of an existing event on the primary Google calendar. Always requires approval.

tools.name: drive_read
tools.description: Read a Google Drive file's text content by file id. Falls back to plain-text export for Google-native docs.

tools.name: drive_create
tools.description: Create a new file in Google Drive with the given name and text content. Always requires approval.

tools.name: drive_update
tools.description: Replace the content of an existing Google Drive file by id. Always requires approval.

tools.name: bg_list
tools.description: List background shell tasks spawned this session. Optionally filter by status (all|running|done|failed).

tools.name: bg_status
tools.description: Check the status and optionally tail the output log of a background task.

id.description: Task id from shell_cmd background run

log.description: Include the last 4000 chars of output (default false)

tools.name: ref_ingest
tools.description: Ingest a reference (URL / file / repo / image / transcript) into durable project context. Stored under ~/.vanta/refs/ and recallable across sessions without re-pasting. Pass an excerpt to skip fetching; or let the tool read the source.

source.description: URL, file path, or repo path to ingest

tools.name: ref_search
tools.description: Search ingested references by keyword. Returns matching refs with their excerpts.

tools.name: ref_list
tools.description: List all ingested references, most recent first.

tools.name: protect
tools.description: Scan text for threats: scams, credential exposure, destructive commands, social engineering, agent-overreach instructions, and contract traps. Use on suspicious messages, contract clauses, or any input that might be risky.

tools.name: retrieve_original
tools.description: Expand a compressed tool output back to its full original. Pass the original_id shown in a [vanta compressed …] footer to read the complete content.

original_id.description: The original_id from a [vanta compressed …] footer.

tools.name: loop
tools.description: Create and manage first-class loops: durable, goal-driven iteration cycles that run stages (discover/plan/execute/evaluate/improve) on a trigger (heartbeat/cron/manual). Use add to register, list/show to inspect, pause/resume/kill to control status, run to fire one iteration as a background process (non-blocking), and escalations to read open blockers. Escalations are surfaced here but only a human can clear them via `vanta loop clear` — the agent must not resolve its own blockers.

goal.description: add: natural-language goal the loop pursues.

trigger.description: add: trigger spec — manual | heartbeat | heartbeat:<N> | cron:"<expr>".

purge.description: kill: if true, delete files instead of marking killed.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a message to a named agent registered on the A2A bus. Returns the agent's reply, or a delivery note when the agent returns no reply.

to.description: The agent id to send to.

tools.name: playbook
tools.description: Cross-session experiential playbook. record: capture a reusable strategy after completing a task. recall: surface matching strategies from prior sessions before tackling a task. list: browse recent plays.

tools.name: mount_mcp
tools.description: Spawn an MCP server process and mount its tools into the active registry. Use to hook in an existing MCP server or one you just scaffolded. Returns the list of tool names registered.

name.description: Unique name for this server (used as tool name prefix mcp_<name>_<tool>)

command.description: Command to spawn the server (e.g. npx, node)

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

env.description: Optional env vars for the server process

tools.name: tool_search
tools.description: Search for tools by name or description keyword. Returns matching tool names + full schemas. Use before calling an unfamiliar tool to verify its parameter shape. When VANTA_MCP_DEFER=1 is set, MCP tool schemas are deferred — call tool_search to fetch them.

query.description: Search query (tool name substring or keyword)

maxResults.description: Max number of results to return (default 5, max 20)

commands.name: help
commands.desc: show this command list

commands.name: clear
commands.desc: start a fresh conversation (keeps the session log)

commands.name: reset
commands.desc: start a fresh conversation (alias of /clear)

commands.name: history
commands.desc: show this conversation's transcript

commands.name: export
commands.desc: export this conversation to a markdown file

commands.name: init
commands.desc: generate .claude/CLAUDE.md project context for future sessions

commands.name: retry
commands.desc: re-run your last message

commands.name: undo
commands.desc: drop the last turn from the conversation

commands.name: rewind
commands.desc: list or restore recent per-edit file checkpoints

commands.name: hooks
commands.desc: list, add, or remove shell hooks in .vanta/hooks.json

commands.name: model
commands.desc: change provider & model — interactive picker

commands.name: effort
commands.desc: set model effort for this session

commands.name: setup
commands.desc: configure the model backend — opens the provider/model picker

commands.name: tools
commands.desc: list available tools

commands.name: skills
commands.desc: list learned + installed skills

commands.name: status
commands.desc: kernel, provider, keys, store health

commands.name: health
commands.desc: capability health — gmail/search/vision/browser/mcp + the exact fix for each gap

commands.name: world
commands.desc: view Vanta's world model — entities + relationships across your systems

commands.name: money
commands.desc: Money OS ledger — offers, prospect pipeline, revenue total

commands.name: radar
commands.desc: Opportunity radar — scored opportunities ranked by pain + buyer signal

commands.name: team
commands.desc: background worker roster — named agents, roles, status, blockers

commands.name: lifesearch
commands.desc: search across Vanta's local stores (world/money/radar/team/errors) — source-cited

commands.name: compartments
commands.desc: Vanta's self-repair body map — compartments + max autonomy per part

commands.name: skeptic
commands.desc: adversarially verify a claim — refute by default, demand evidence

commands.name: locks
commands.desc: regression locks — verified behaviors + passing/regressed status (verification organ)

commands.name: reach
commands.desc: internet-reach doctor — each channel's active backend + status + the exact fix per gap

commands.name: cookie
commands.desc: show login-walled reach channels with a stored cookie + the export guide

commands.name: goals
commands.desc: active goals from the kernel

commands.name: goal
commands.desc: set / resume / drop a standing goal (a carried goal starts paused)

commands.name: plan
commands.desc: show the agent's current task plan (todo list)

commands.name: memory
commands.desc: tell Vanta something to remember (→ its brain)

commands.name: compress
commands.desc: compact the conversation context now

commands.name: compact
commands.desc: compact context now (alias of /compress; optional steer)

commands.name: sessions
commands.desc: list saved sessions

commands.name: resume
commands.desc: load a past session into this conversation

commands.name: title
commands.desc: name the current session

commands.name: fork
commands.desc: branch the current conversation into a new session

commands.name: cron
commands.desc: list scheduled tasks

commands.name: image
commands.desc: attach an image for your next message

commands.name: paste
commands.desc: attach an image from the clipboard (macOS)

commands.name: attachments
commands.desc: show or clear pending image attachments

commands.name: branch
commands.desc: create or switch git branch (kernel-gated)

commands.name: context
commands.desc: visual context-budget breakdown

commands.name: files
commands.desc: list files in the current conversation context

commands.name: composer
commands.desc: input box position: float (default) or bottom-pinned

commands.name: cockpit
commands.desc: open mission-control — kernel verdict ladder, goals, loops (TUI)

commands.name: loops
commands.desc: live loops + escalations dashboard (TUI)

commands.name: changes
commands.desc: review changed files — per-file keep/undo (TUI)

commands.name: mcp
commands.desc: list configured MCP servers

commands.name: permissions
commands.desc: tighten-only tool permission rules (~/.vanta/permissions.tsv)

commands.name: preferences
commands.desc: export operator preference signals (~/.vanta/preferences.jsonl)

commands.name: usage
commands.desc: token usage + context fill for this session

commands.name: copy
commands.desc: copy the last response to the clipboard

commands.name: update
commands.desc: git pull the latest Vanta (then ./install.sh to rebuild)

commands.name: moim
commands.desc: pin a top-of-mind note injected into every prompt until cleared

commands.name: output-style
commands.desc: control response verbosity

commands.name: ultrathink
commands.desc: deep-reasoning mode — plan, weigh tradeoffs + edge cases, then act

commands.name: ultracode
commands.desc: multi-agent coding push — decompose, parallel subagents, verify, synthesize

commands.name: deep-research
commands.desc: fan-out research — multi-source search, skeptic-verify, cited synthesis

commands.name: next
commands.desc: surface one concrete next micro-step from your active goals

commands.name: now
commands.desc: execute items in the Now column — agent picks up whatever you put there

commands.name: planmode
commands.desc: enforced plan mode — write tools blocked until /planmode approve

commands.name: boundary
commands.desc: mark a task boundary — archive the current task state and begin fresh

commands.name: where
commands.desc: show last stated intent and recent tool call breadcrumb

commands.name: wm
commands.desc: view or add to session working memory (cleared each session)

commands.name: restart
commands.desc: reload Vanta in place with fresh code (needs ./run.sh)

commands.name: bug
commands.desc: record a structured bug with session + git context

commands.name: handoff
commands.desc: copy-paste handoff packet (goals, git, files, next step)

commands.name: open
commands.desc: open a file:line in your editor ($VANTA_EDITOR/code)

commands.name: edit
commands.desc: edit the last AI response in your editor ($VANTA_EDITOR/code)

commands.name: tasks
commands.desc: operator task stack — /tasks next for the best move

commands.name: btw
commands.desc: ask a quick side question — not added to conversation history

commands.name: diff
commands.desc: show uncommitted changes (working tree + staged)

commands.name: search
commands.desc: search conversation history for a term

commands.name: repro
commands.desc: save a diagnostic repro bundle to .vanta/repro-*.md

commands.name: summary
commands.desc: summarize this session (what was done, what remains)

commands.name: dashboard
commands.desc: live operator state — tasks, goals, repo, model

commands.name: brief
commands.desc: JARVIS-style today brief — tasks, goals, calendar, episodic

commands.name: review
commands.desc: review changed code for bugs and cleanups (low|medium|high)

commands.name: simplify
commands.desc: reuse/simplify/efficiency/altitude pass on changed code

commands.name: auto
commands.desc: auto-minimalism mode — do the least that works (stdlib>deps, deletion>addition); 'review' audits a diff for deletable code

commands.name: verify
commands.desc: run the app and confirm a change actually works

commands.name: run
commands.desc: launch and drive this project's app

commands.name: add-dir
commands.desc: add a directory to this session's readable/writable scope

commands.name: routes
commands.desc: show provider routing config (main + named VANTA_ROUTE_* overrides)

commands.name: rename
commands.desc: rename the current session

commands.name: tui
commands.desc: TUI renderer info; /tui fullscreen confirms alt-screen mode

commands.name: focus
commands.desc: toggle focus view — hide tool events, show only user + final responses

commands.name: config
commands.desc: show resolved config (provider, model, kernel, dirs)

commands.name: settings
commands.desc: show/edit persisted settings

commands.name: models
commands.desc: list available models for the active provider

commands.name: lint
commands.desc: run the code-size gate on changed files

commands.name: roadmap
commands.desc: open the drag-and-drop roadmap board

commands.name: audit
commands.desc: run npm audit + dependency checks

commands.name: today
commands.desc: today brief — tasks, goals, calendar, episodic

commands.name: import
commands.desc: import config/skills from a backup archive

commands.name: exit
commands.desc: leave the session
